meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no version control (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to stop losing progress. A beginner-friendly checklist to master version control using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop relying on folder copies like 'my-app-v2'. Version control is your safety net. By integrating Git with AI agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you automate commit messages, branch management, and conflict resolution. This transforms your workflow from chaotic experimentation into a professional, portfolio-ready development pipeline.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: 3 AM, your code is broken, and you’ve overwritten the one file that actually worked. Without version control, your project is a house of cards waiting for a stiff breeze. You aren't just losing code; you are losing the ability to experiment without fear.

But the era of manual backups is dead. By leveraging AI-driven version control, you turn Git from a daunting CLI hurdle into a silent partner. This guide bridges the gap, showing you how to harness Antigravity and Claude Code to automate your history, secure your progress, and build like a senior engineer from day one.

steps.title: Initialize Your Repository with Intent
steps.description: Before writing code, you must establish a source of truth. We initialize Git to track every keystroke, ensuring you can always roll back to a known working state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional Git repository for a new web project, including a .gitignore file optimized for Node.js and environment variables.

steps.title: Automate Commit Documentation
steps.description: The hardest part of Git is writing meaningful commit messages. Use AI to generate semantic, descriptive logs that explain 'why' you changed the code, not just 'what'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current staged changes and generate a concise, conventional-commit style message that follows best practices.

steps.title: Branching for Safe Experimentation
steps.description: Never push experimental features to your main branch. Create feature branches to isolate your work and keep your production-ready code stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new branch named 'feature/authentication' and switch to it, ensuring the transition is clean.

steps.title: Resolving Merge Conflicts with AI
steps.description: Merge conflicts are inevitable. Instead of manually parsing cryptic Git markers, use AI to identify the correct logic and merge the branches seamlessly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I have a merge conflict in main.js. Review the HEAD and the feature branch changes, then suggest the resolved code block.

steps.title: Automated Pull Request Summaries
steps.description: When you are ready to merge, provide a high-level summary of your changes. This is essential for your portfolio and team collaboration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional Pull Request description detailing the additions, bug fixes, and impact of the changes in this branch.

pro_tips: Use Git hooks to trigger AI-linting every time you commit to ensure your code quality never dips.
Always commit small, logical chunks rather than massive file dumps; it makes debugging via AI significantly more accurate.
Tag your releases using semantic versioning (v1.0.0) to track your project's evolution for your portfolio.
Keep your .gitignore updated to prevent sensitive API keys from ever touching your repository history.
Treat your commit history as a story; future employers will read it to understand your problem-solving process.
content.next_steps: Once you have mastered local version control, push your repositories to GitHub and start documenting your project milestones to demonstrate your growth to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the power to track, revert, and document your code like a professional. Stop coding in the dark and start building a legacy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
